action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|.S.#..#..|
 2|.........|
 3|...#B#...|
 4|A###.....|
 5|...##.q.#|
 6|......#x#|
 7|.#y...#.#|
 8|2......#.|
 9|1#..p#...|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (2,1) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (2,1), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (1,4): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {1}. So it is open at t=1,3,... and closed at t=0,2,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (5,3): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {1}. So it is open at t=1,3,... and closed at t=0,2,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (5,9); t mod 4=1: (4,9); t mod 4=2: (3,9); t mod 4=3: (4,9). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (7,5); t mod 4=1: (6,5); t mod 4=2: (6,6); t mod 4=3: (6,5). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (1,9) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (8,6) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (1,8) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (3,7) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 45 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 9 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive only a per-tick log of OK/BUMP/CAUGHT/PICKED UP/DELIVERED. You are NEVER told where you are or why you bumped - track your position yourself.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am at (2,1) at t=0. I need to deliver Parcel 1 (Pickup: 1,9 -> Delivery: 8,6) and Parcel 2 (Pickup: 2,8 -> Delivery: 3,7).
Guard P cycle (len 4): t=0:(5,9), t=1:(4,9), t=2:(3,9), t=3:(4,9).
Guard Q cycle (len 4): t=0:(7,5), t=1:(6,5), t=2:(6,6), t=3:(6,5).
Gate A (1,4) and Gate B (5,3) are open at odd t (1, 3, 5...) and closed at even t (0, 2, 4...).
My immediate goal is to pick up Parcel 1 at (1,9). The path from (2,1) to (1,9) is clear of walls and guards initially.
Path: (2,1) -> (2,2) -> (2,3) -> (2,4) -> (2,5) -> (2,6) -> (2,7) -> (2,8) -> (2,9) -> (1,9).
Let's check guard positions along this path at the arrival times:
t=1: Arrive (2,2). P(4,9), Q(6,5). Safe.
t=2: Arrive (2,3). P(3,9), Q(6,6). Safe.
t=3: Arrive (2,4). P(4,9), Q(6,5). Safe.
t=4: Arrive (2,5). P(5,9), Q(6,5). Safe.
t=5: Arrive (2,6). P(3,9), Q(6,6). Safe.
t=6: Arrive (2,7). P(4,9), Q(6,5). Safe.
t=7: Arrive (2,8). P(5,9), Q(6,5). Safe.
t=8: Arrive (2,9). P(3,9), Q(6,6). Safe.
t=9: Arrive (1,9). P(4,9), Q(6,5). Safe.
This direct vertical path works perfectly for 9 ticks. I will commit to this first plan.
Note: At t=8, I am at (2,9). At t=9, I move W to (1,9) and pick up Parcel 1.
Wait, I need to check if I can do this in 5 moves. No, the distance is 8 steps. I must take multiple plans.
Plan 1: Move South 5 times.
(2,1) -> (2,2) -> (2,3) -> (2,4) -> (2,5) -> (2,6).
Arrival at (2,6) at t=5. Safe.
Next, I need to go further down.
Plan 2: Move South 4 times.
(2,6) -> (2,7) -> (2,8) -> (2,9) -> (1,9).
Arrival at (1,9) at t=9. Pick up Parcel 1.
This seems efficient. Let's start with Plan 1.

POSITION: (2,1)
PLAN: S S S S S
action.label: response

action.content:
ROUND 1 RESULT (ticks 0 -> 5):
t=1: S -> OK.
t=2: S -> OK.
t=3: S -> BUMP.
t=4: S -> BUMP.
t=5: S -> BUMP.
STATUS: tick 5 of 45. Delivered 0 of 2 parcel(s). Rounds left: 8. (You are never told your position - track it yourself.)
Respond now in the required format: a POSITION line predicting your cell after your next plan, then a PLAN line with exactly 5 moves.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: My previous plan assumed a clear path South, but the log shows BUMPS at t=3, 4, and 5. This means there are walls or guards blocking the direct vertical path at (2,3), (2,4), and (2,5). Looking at the map:
Row 3: `...#B#...` -> There is a wall at (4,3) and a Gate B at (5,3). The column 4 has a wall at row 3.
Row 4: `A###.....` -> There is a wall at (2,4), (3,4), (4,4).
Ah, I see. At t=3, I tried to move S from (2,2) to (2,3). The log says BUMP. Let's re-examine the map at (2,3).
Map Row 3: `...#B#...`
Columns: 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9
Chars: . . . # B # . . .
So (2,3) is '.', (3,3) is '.', (4,3) is '#', (5,3) is 'B'.
Wait, if (2,3) is '.', why did I bump?
Let's re-read the map carefully.
Row 1: `.S.#..#..` -> (2,1) is S. (4,1) is #. (7,1) is #.
Row 2: `.........` -> All clear.
Row 3: `...#B#...` -> (4,3) is #, (5,3) is B, (6,3) is #.
Row 4: `A###.....` -> (1,4) is A, (2,4) is #, (3,4) is #, (4,4) is #.
Row 5: `...##.q.#` -> (4,5) is #, (5,5) is #, (7,5) is Q, (9,5) is #.
Row 6: `......#x#` -> (7,6) is #, (8,6) is x, (9,6) is #.
Row 7: `.#y...#.#` -> (1,7) is #, (2,7) is ., (3,7) is y, (7,7) is #, (9,7) is #.
Row 8: `2......#.` -> (1,8) is 2, (8,8) is #.
Row 9: `1#..p#...` -> (1,9) is 1, (2,9) is #, (4,9) is P, (6,9) is #.

Okay, let's re-trace my first move.
Start (2,1) at t=0.
Plan: S S S S S.
t=1: Move S to (2,2). Map (2,2) is '.'. OK.
t=2: Move S to (2,3). Map (2,3) is '.'. OK.
t=3: Move S to (2,4). Map (2,4) is '#' (from Row 4 `A###.....`).
So at t=3, I tried to move into a wall at (2,4). This is a BUMP. I stay at (2,3).
t=4: Move S from (2,3) to (2,4). Still a wall. BUMP. Stay at (2,3).
t=5: Move S from (2,3) to (2,4). Still a wall. BUMP. Stay at (2,3).

So after 5 ticks, I am at (2,3) holding 0 parcels.
Now I need to go around the wall at (2,4). The wall is horizontal in row 4 from col 2 to 4.
To get to the bottom (towards row 9 where Parcel 1 is), I must go around the wall.
Path options:
1. Go West to (1,3), then Down to (1,4) (Gate A), then Down to (1,5)...
2. Go East to (3,3), then Down to (3,4) (Wall!), so that doesn't work.
3. Go East to (3,3), then East to (4,3) (Wall!), so that doesn't work.
So the only way down from row 3 is through (1,3) -> (1,4) -> (1,5).
Let's check the path:
(2,3) -> (1,3) [West] -> (1,4) [South, Gate A] -> (1,5) [South] -> (1,6) [South] -> (1,7) [South].
Let's verify obstacles and guards on this new path.
Current Pos: (2,3) at t=5.
Guard P at t=5: 5 mod 4 = 1 -> (4,9).
Guard Q at t=5: 5 mod 4 = 1 -> (6,5).
Gate A (1,4): Open at odd t (1, 3, 5...). Closed at even t (0, 2, 4...).
I need to step onto (1,4) at an odd t.
Current t=5 (odd). If I move S now, I arrive at t=6 (even). Gate A is closed. BUMP.
So I must WAIT at t=5 to make t=6 even? No, I need t+1 to be odd. So I need t to be even.
Current t=5. Next even t is 6. So I need to wait 1 tick to reach t=6.
At t=6, I move S to (1,4). t=7 (odd). Gate A is open. OK.
Then continue down.
Let's plan the next 5 moves starting from (2,3) at t=5.
Moves: W, S, WAIT, S, S.
t=5: W to (1,3). OK.
t=6: S to (1,4). Arrive t=7. Gate A open? Yes (7 is odd). OK.
t=7: S to (1,5). OK.
t=8: S to (1,6). OK.
t=9: S to (1,7). OK.
This plan gets me to (1,7) at t=9.
From (1,7), I need to go to (1,9) to pick up Parcel 1.
(1,7) -> (1,8) -> (1,9).
Check guards at t=9: P(4,9), Q(6,5). Safe.
t=10: S to (1,8). OK.
t=11: S to (1,9). Pick up Parcel 1. OK.
So the strategy is:
1. Go around the wall via Gate A.
2. Pick up Parcel 1.
3. Go to (8,6) to deliver Parcel 1.
4. Go to (1,8) to pick up Parcel 2.
5. Go to (3,7) to deliver Parcel 2.

Let's refine the first plan to ensure safety.
Start: (2,3) at t=5.
Plan: W S WAIT S S
t=5: W -> (1,3). Guard P(4,9), Q(6,5). Safe.
t=6: S -> (1,4). Arrive t=7. Gate A open (7%2!=0). Safe.
t=7: S -> (1,5). Safe.
t=8: S -> (1,6). Safe.
t=9: S -> (1,7). Safe.
Position after plan: (1,7).

POSITION: (1,7)
PLAN: S S S S S

  - t=6: S -> (1,4).
